name: Fon
notes: Fon (fɔ̀ngbè) — Gbe (Atlantic-Congo) language of Benin. Written in a Latin-based alphabet following the Africa Alphabet, with letters such as ɖ, ɛ, ɔ and digraphs for prenasalised and labialised consonants, plus obligatory tone diacritics (Fon is a tonal language with contrastive high/low and contour tones). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec must jointly model the segmental letters, nasal vowels and the tone-marking system, which requires dedicated sourcing. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin-script Fon orthography with tone diacritics. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode fonn1241), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode fonn1241.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
